zh.lifeSummary: Sybil Vessey是Thomas Vessey的女兒，於1561年11月15日出生在英國艾可蕾托 巴多福。她於1587年9月15日與John Porter在英國艾可蕾托 巴多福結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1625年11月26日在英國英格蘭艾可費爾斯泰德享年64歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可費爾斯泰德。

nameMeanings.name: Vessey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vessey (Manche), named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Vessius or Vettius (of uncertain origin) + the locative suffix -acum. There has been some confusion with Veazey .

variants.name: Veazey
meanings: English (Rutland): nickname from Anglo-Norman French enveisé ‘playful’ (Old French envoisié), Latinized as invesiatus (compare medieval Latin invasus ‘possessed by a demon’) and paralleled by lascivus ‘wanton’. The first syllable was lost early, resulting in numerous dialectal and orthographic variants; only Lenfestey preserves the fuller form, with an intrusive medial -t-. There has also been some confusion with Vessey .
History: John Veazey came from England to MD in the late 17th century. Thomas Ward Veazey (born 1774) was a MD legislator and planter.

nameMeanings.name: Sybil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybilla
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Sybil Vessey was born on 15 November 1561, in Little Baddow, Essex, England as the daughter of Thomas Vessey. She married John Porter on 15 September 1587, in Little Baddow, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 26 November 1625, in Felsted, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in Felsted, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Sybil Vessey est née le 15 novembre 1561, à Little Baddow, Essex, Angleterre, fille de Thomas Vessey. Elle a épousé John Porter le 15 septembre 1587, à Little Baddow, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 26 novembre 1625, à Felsted, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Felsted, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sybil Vesseyは1561年11月15日にイギリスエセックスリットル バッドオウでThomas Vesseyの娘として生まれた。1587年9月15日にイギリスエセックスリットル バッドオウでJohn Porterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1625年11月26日にイギリスイギリスエセックスフエルステドで64歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスフエルステドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sybil Vessey(은)는 1561년 11월 15일 영국 에세스 리틀 바도에서 Thomas Vessey의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1587년 9월 15일 영국 에세스 리틀 바도에서 John Porter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1625년 11월 26일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 프스테드에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 프스테드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sybil Vessey родилась 15 ноября 1561 г. в местности литл баддоу, эссекс, Англия. Её отца звали Thomas Vessey. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Porter 15 сентября 1587 г. в местности литл баддоу, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 26 ноября 1625 г. в местности фелстед, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности фелстед, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
